brief_document.title: Server schedule endpoints and integration test
brief_document.purpose: Expose schedule management via gRPC and HTTP endpoints in aion-server, and provide an end-to-end integration test proving the full schedule lifecycle: create, fire on interval, overlap prevention, recovery re-arm.
brief_document.task: Add schedule management endpoints to aion-server (gRPC and HTTP): CreateSchedule, UpdateSchedule, PauseSchedule, ResumeSchedule, DeleteSchedule, ListSchedules, DescribeSchedule. Each endpoint delegates to the Engine methods. Write an integration test that creates a schedule, verifies it fires, verifies overlap policy prevents concurrent executions, and verifies recovery re-arms timers after restart.

requirements.title: gRPC schedule endpoints
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add gRPC service methods for schedule management matching the Engine API: CreateSchedule, UpdateSchedule, PauseSchedule, ResumeSchedule, DeleteSchedule, ListSchedules, DescribeSchedule.
acceptance: Proto definitions exist for all seven schedule RPCs.
Server handlers delegate to Engine methods.
Each RPC returns appropriate error codes for invalid input and not-found cases.

requirements.title: HTTP schedule endpoints
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add HTTP REST endpoints for schedule management: POST /schedules, PUT /schedules/:id, POST /schedules/:id/pause, POST /schedules/:id/resume, DELETE /schedules/:id, GET /schedules, GET /schedules/:id.
acceptance: All seven HTTP endpoints exist and delegate to Engine methods.
Responses use appropriate HTTP status codes (201 for create, 404 for not found, etc.).

requirements.title: Integration test: schedule lifecycle with recovery
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL have an integration test that: creates a schedule with a short interval, verifies it fires and starts a workflow, verifies overlap policy (Skip) prevents a concurrent start, and verifies that after simulated recovery the schedule re-arms and fires again.
acceptance: Schedule creation succeeds and returns a ScheduleId.
Within the expected interval, a workflow is started with the schedule's configured input.
With overlap policy Skip and a still-running workflow, the next trigger is skipped.
After recovery, the schedule re-arms and fires again.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement WebSocket streaming for schedule events — that is a future enhancement.
SHALL NOT implement dashboard UI for schedules — that belongs in the dashboard cluster.

constraints.text: Schedule state is projected from events, never stored as mutable fields.
Schedule evaluation uses durable timers (AT infrastructure), not wall-clock polling.
Overlap and catch-up policies are explicit per-schedule configuration, not implicit engine behavior.
Recovery must reconstruct schedule state from events and re-arm timers for all active schedules.
A deleted schedule's events are retained for audit but the schedule is no longer evaluated.
Schedule mutations are serialized per ScheduleId to prevent concurrent modification races.

resolved_context.intention: Production workflow engines must support time-triggered execution: run this billing workflow every midnight, check this API every 5 minutes, generate this report every Monday at 9am. Without built-in scheduling, every user must build their own cron-to-workflow bridge, duplicating coordination logic (overlap prevention, catch-up, pause/resume) that belongs in the engine. This cluster adds a Schedule resource to Aion: a persistent, durable configuration that starts workflow executions on a cron expression or fixed interval, with policies for overlap, catch-up on missed triggers, and pause/resume. Schedules are engine-level — they survive restarts, are queryable, and their trigger history is auditable.
